title: Travis Egedy
role_summary: Denver musician (Pictureplane); scene peer from the Rhinoceropolis era. Met Colin when Colin was 15 and Travis was 19. Dedicated "Color Spectrum (Tokyo Drift)" on Degenerate (2018) to Colin.
body:
Travis Egedy (Pictureplane) lived at Rhinoceropolis for six years until 2012. He and Colin knew each other for a long time — they met when Colin was fifteen and Egedy was nineteen. After Colin's death, Egedy released the album *Degenerate* (September 2018, Alien Body Music label) and dedicated the track "Color Spectrum (Tokyo Drift)" — feat. smrtdeath — to Colin, referencing Colin's online alias "Tokyo Drift." The music video is a tribute to Colin's spirit and artistic presence at Rhinoceropolis. Per a Westword interview: "Both lyrically and visually, the song and music video are tributes to Ward, a friend and cohort of Egedy's, whose spirit and artistic presence shaped Rhinoceropolis."

## Sources
- [Westword — Pictureplane's Travis Egedy Remembers Rhinoceropolis and Colin Ward (2018)](https://www.westword.com/music/pictureplanes-travis-egedy-remembers-rhinoceropolis-and-colin-ward-in-new-music-video-11031024)
- [Pictureplane Bandcamp — Color Spectrum (Tokyo Drift)](https://pictureplane.bandcamp.com/track/color-spectrum-tokyo-drift-feat-smrt-death)
